skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
stem: Consider the system of equations: y = 2x − 1 and y = x2. How many solutions are there to this system of equations?
rationale: Setting x2 = 2x − 1 gives x2 − 2x + 1 = 0, i.e. (x − 1)2 = 0. The discriminant is (−2)2 − 4(1)(1) = 0, so there is exactly one (repeated) real solution, x = 1, corresponding to the tangent point (1, 1). Hence the system has exactly one solution.
